Are you on the hunt for the perfect gift for the surfer in your life?
Explore our surf gift guide, where you’ll find a treasure trove of options ranging from essential gear, practical accessories, gift cards to fund their next surfing escapade, and much more.
Whether you’re shopping for a seasoned wave rider or an enthusiastic beginner, our surfing gift ideas are designed to cater to all levels, ensuring their next session is unforgettable.
There’s lots to discuss, so let’s jump to it!
Essential Surfing Gear

body.alt: An image of surfboards in a shop

children.text: First, let’s start with the basics: essential surfing gear.

Every surfer understands that having the right gear enhances performance and ensures safety in the water. Quality surfboards provide buoyancy and stability, while high-performance wetsuits keep surfers warm and protected.
…And let’s not forget the trusty surfboard leash that keeps the board close during those unexpected wipeouts.
With that said, let’s take a look at these surfing gift ideas in more detail:
Best Surfboards for Beginners

Selecting the right surfboard is crucial for beginners to ensure a positive learning experience on the waves. That’s why a beginner-friendly surfboard makes a fantastic gift for someone just starting out.

Beginners benefit from larger surfboards, usually around 8 to 9 feet, due to their stability and buoyancy. Longboards and foam surfboards are particularly favoured by novices because they provide better balance and are easier to paddle.

Soft-top boards, which are more forgiving and reduce the risk of injury, are also excellent for beginners. These high-volume surfboards help newbies gain confidence, making learning more enjoyable for them.
High-Performance Wetsuits

Wetsuits are essential for maintaining body temperature in cold water, keeping surfers warm and comfortable. A snug fit minimises water entry and maximises insulation, enhancing the wetsuit’s effectiveness. Quality neoprene material is preferred for its durability and flexibility, providing a better fit and greater comfort.

Investing in a high-performance wetsuit is crucial for enjoying surfing in various weather conditions. Whether it’s a gift for a seasoned surfer or someone new to the sport, a good wetsuit can make a significant difference in their surfing experience.
Reliable Surfboard Leashes
A high-quality leash is a practical and thoughtful gift idea for any surfer. It allows them to enjoy their time in the water without worrying about losing their board during a wipeout.
Typically, leashes should be 1 to 2 feet longer than the surfboard to ensure optimal performance.
Where the thickness of the leash is concerned, thicker leashes (7mm or more) are more durable, making them better suited for bigger waves and more powerful surf.
In contrast, thinner leashes (5mm to 6mm) create less drag and are ideal for smaller waves and performance surfing.
So, consider what kind of surfing your gift recipient gets up to and allow that to guide your purchase.
Practical Surfing Accessories

body.alt: An image of a rapture camps towel

children.text: Practical surfing accessories can make a world of difference for any wave rider. Consider gifting specialised changing towels for hassle-free outfit swaps, a waterproof dry bag to keep valuables safe, or a surfboard storage solution for easy organisation.
These thoughtful surfing gifts will enhance their surfing adventures and show how much you care.
Let’s dive deeper into these gift ideas and see how they improve a surfer’s experience:
Changing Towels and Mats
A changing mat and changing towels provide surfers with a clean, comfortable and private way to change at the beach.

Fleece-lined and showerproof changing towels are particularly beneficial for keeping warm and dry in colder weather. These robes are designed with oversized sleeves, allowing easy movement while changing.
Ear Protection
High-quality earplugs are essential for preventing surfer’s ear and other ear-related issues.
Fortunately, there are earplugs available that are explicitly designed to protect against cold water and wind while allowing surfers to hear critical sounds around them.
These earplugs are often made from advanced materials to ensure a comfortable, secure fit, so they stay in place during intense wave sessions.

Investing in high-quality ear protection for the surfer in your life can enhance their surfing experience and protect their hearing health long-term, allowing them to enjoy the waves for years to come.
Waterproof Dry Bags

Waterproof dry bags are great for surfers who want to keep their personal belongings safe and dry while enjoying the waves. Whether heading out for a quick session or spending an entire day at the beach, a reliable dry bag can protect their valuables from water damage, sand, and moisture.

Smaller bags are perfect for protecting their stuff like a phone, wallet and snacks for when they’re out on the water. In contrast, larger ones can accommodate wetsuits, towels, and a change of clothes. Some dry bags also feature backpack straps or handles for easy carrying, making them versatile for hiking to a remote surf spot.
Surfboard Racks and Storage
Surfboard racks help organise and protect surfboards when they’re not in use. Proper storage solutions extend the lifespan of surfboards by protecting them from environmental elements.

Wall-mounted surfboard racks are a space-saving option that keeps boards easily accessible while maintaining their condition. So, if the surfer in your life is struggling to store their gear, this could be a great gift idea!
Water Bottles
Staying hydrated when out on the waves is essential, so a reusable water bottle is an excellent gift for surfers of all abilities.
Take
Rapture Surfcamps’ water bottle
, for instance. These bottles are lightweight and durable, making them easy to carry and resistant to damage during beach trips.

Plus, they’re reusable, helping surfers reduce plastic waste—something lots of surfers are super passionate about! Additionally, the bottle’s stylish design aligns with surf culture, making it both a functional and fashionable accessory for any surfer.
…and for only €10.00, this water bottle is an excellent stocking filler or token gift!
Beach Towels
An often-overlooked gift – but an essential accessory for any surfer, is the humble beach towel.
Rapture Surfcamps offers a couple of options
; either is excellent for several reasons.

For starters, their beach towels are crafted from high-quality, absorbent material, allowing surfers to dry off quickly after catching waves. The towels’ generous size provides ample space for surfers to stretch out and relax on the beach between surf sessions. This also means they offer plenty of coverage for wrapping up after riding the waves, making them versatile for various beach activities.
Surfing Gadgets and Tech

body.alt: An image of a surfer's hand he is wearing a watch

children.text: From action cameras that capture high-energy moments to smartwatches that monitor surf conditions and fitness, these gadgets add a modern twist to surfing adventures. Let’s take a closer look:
Action Cameras

Action cameras are a must-have for capturing memorable surfing moments. Many models boast excellent image stabilisation, high-quality video capture, and extended battery life, enabling surfers to document their rides in stunning detail.
Whether they’re vlogging or simply preserving their surfing adventures, an action camera ensures they won’t miss a moment.
Smartwatches for Surfers
Investing in a smartwatch designed for surfers is a thoughtful gift that combines practicality with cutting-edge technology.
Smartwatches tailored for surfers often include features like tide tracking and fitness monitoring, which enhance safety and provide the information surfers need to level up their performance.
Surfing Apparel and Lifestyle

body.alt: An image of a surfer wearing a rapturecamps hoodie

children.text: Surfing apparel is more than just gear—it’s a statement of the surfing lifestyle. With a variety of options that seamlessly blend style and practicality, surfers can express themselves both on and off the waves.
Whether they’re hitting the beach in a sleek rash vest or enjoying the sunset in casual, eco-friendly attire, surf clothing offers the perfect mix of functionality and fashion.
Let’s take a closer look…
Stylish Rash Vests

Rash vests provide sun protection and comfort by preventing chafing and providing UPF ratings, making them a must-have for any surfer. Modern rash vests are designed for enhanced comfort and flexibility, ensuring that surfers can focus on riding the waves without any distractions.
Casual Wear for Surfers

Casual wear for surfers includes trendy t-shirts, hoodies, and caps that reflect surfing culture. Whether it’s a laid-back day at the beach or hanging out with friends, casual surf wear keeps the vibe cool and relaxed. Check Rapture Surfcamp’s range of
awesome casualwear here
Eco-Friendly Clothing

Eco-friendly clothing is popular among surfers who want to minimise their environmental impact. Typically, these items are made from a range of sustainable materials like organic cotton and/or recycled polyester, ensuring that surfers can enjoy their lifestyle while protecting the planet.
Framed Prints
Surfers tend to have a deep-rooted appreciation for the ocean and nature, so a
beautifully framed poster
can help capture the spirit of surfing and the serenity of the sea, making it a perfect decoration for their home or surf shack.
Check out our surf camps!
Unique and Personalised Surf Gifts

Unique and personalised items that reflect a surfer’s passion make for truly memorable gifts—custom surf maps, surf-themed jewellery, and customised artwork are all great examples of creative and sentimental gifts that we’re sure the recipient will love!
A Custom Surf Map

Did you know you can order custom surf maps highlighting your surfer’s favourite surf spots? These maps not only showcase the locations where they’ve caught your best waves but also serve as stunning, unique home decor pieces.
These maps can be customised to include details like the names of the surf breaks, dates of their memorable sessions, and even photographs.
They make perfect gifts for surfers and ocean enthusiasts, offering a personal and artistic way to celebrate their passion for the sea.
Surf-Themed Jewellery

Surf-themed jewellery, such as wave pendants or surfboard charms, stylishly reflects a surfer’s passion. These items not only enhance personal style but also convey meaningful connections to the surfing lifestyle, which is why they’re excellent gifts for surfers who want to keep a piece of the ocean with them at all times.
Gift Vouchers and Subscriptions

Gift vouchers and subscriptions offer surfers flexibility and long-lasting enjoyment. Whether it’s a voucher for surfing lessons or a subscription to a surfing magazine, these options make excellent gifts that can be personalised to suit the recipient’s preferences.
Surfing Lesson Vouchers

Surfing lesson vouchers are ideal for both beginners and experienced surfers. These lessons can increase a surfer’s confidence in the water, help them understand waves better, and promote safety. Many surf schools, including
Rapture Surfcamps, offer gift vouchers
, making them a practical gift option.
Magazine Subscriptions

Magazine subscriptions keep surfers updated on the latest trends, tips, and news in the surfing world. Popular subscriptions like Carve offer stunning photography, travel features, and in-depth articles that can inspire and inform surfers of all ages. It really is the gift that keeps on giving, providing regular doses of surfing inspiration and information.
Are You Ready to Buy the Surfer In Your Life a Gift?

Finding the perfect gifts for surfers involves understanding their unique needs and passions. Whether you’re considering essential gear like surfboards and wetsuits, practical accessories, high-tech gadgets, or stylish apparel, there’s something to suit every surfer’s lifestyle. Gift vouchers and subscriptions can also provide flexible and ongoing enjoyment.

If you’ve made your way to Tamarindo for the waves (and those incredible sunsets), you’ll also want to know another aspect of this laid-back paradise that deserves your attention: the food scene. Whether you’re looking to load up on carbs before an early morning session, refuel after hours of catching waves or celebrate that perfect ride with some beachside dining, we’ve got you covered with some top Costa Rica food.
Grab your board, work up an appetite, and let’s dive into Tamarindo’s culinary offerings. The surf’s up, and dinner is served.
Early Morning Fuel-Ups
Catching those glassy morning waves means an early start, but don’t worry as Tamarindo has some great options for early risers looking to get some food in Costa Rica.
Café Tico
This
local favorite

opens early and serves up some of the best coffee in town. Their breakfast burritos are packed with eggs, cheese, and your choice of bacon or chorizo—perfect for fueling up before hitting the waves. Pair it with their strong Costa Rican coffee, and you’ll be ready to tackle even the biggest swells.
Tip: Grab one of their homemade energy bars for a mid-session snack. They’re packed with nuts and dried fruit, giving you a quick energy boost between sets.
Santa Rita Café

body.alt: An image of an acai bowl

children.text: For health-conscious surfers,
Santa Rita Café
is the perfect morning pit stop. This charming spot opens early, serving up a menu full of fresh, locally-sourced fuel for your dawn patrol.

Their açaí bowls are legendary – packed with antioxidants and topped with tropical fruits and crunchy granola, they’re the ideal pre-surf breakfast. Pair it with their locally-sourced coffee for a real wake-up call.

If you need something heartier, the avocado toast topped with a poached egg will keep you riding waves all morning. In a rush? Grab a ‘Green Machine’ smoothie to go – it’s like a liquid multivitamin that actually tastes good.
Don’t miss: homemade energy balls, perfect for stashing in your board shorts for a mid-session boost.
Post-Surf Refuel Stations
After hours in the water, you’ll need to replenish those calories. Here are some great spots to satisfy your post-surf hunger.
Surf Shack
A casual spot popular with local surfers,
Surf Shack’s

portions are generous, prices are reasonable, and the atmosphere is relaxed. The burgers here are a local favorite. Think juicy patties topped with fresh ingredients and you’re not far off. It’s a great place to refuel after a long session in the water.
Don’t miss: Their all-day breakfast menu. Eggs any style, bacon, pancakes, and bottomless coffee make for a perfect post-surf meal.
Green Papaya

body.alt: An image of fish tacos

children.text: If you’re looking for something lighter after surfing,
Green Papaya

is worth a visit. They’re known for their fresh fish tacos, made with locally caught fish and topped with tangy slaw and house-made salsas. Pair it with one of their fresh-pressed juices for a refreshing and healthy post-surf meal.
Surfer’s special: Ask about their daily specials – they often feature unique combinations of local ingredients.
Beachfront Bites
Sometimes, you just want to stay close to the waves. Luckily, Tamarindo has plenty of spots where you can enjoy a meal with your toes in the sand.
Lola’s
A short trip from Tamarindo,
Lola’s

at Playa Avellanas is worth the journey. This beachfront restaurant is famous among surfers and food lovers alike. Their wood-fired pizzas are crispy and delicious, and the fish tacos are some of the best in the area. The best part? You can watch the surf while you eat, with a cold drink in hand.
Local’s tip: Try to snag a table right on the beach for the best views of the surf.
Nogui’s
Located right on Tamarindo beach,
Nogui’s

is perfect for a sunset dinner after a day of surfing. They’re known for their fresh seafood, always caught locally and expertly prepared. Their ceviche is a must-try—tangy, spicy, and made with the catch of the day. It’s the perfect light meal when you’re too sun-kissed for anything heavy.
Surfer’s choice: Try their grilled fish of the day. It’s simply prepared, allowing the freshness of the fish to shine through.
Healthy Options
Surfing is demanding, and keeping your body in top shape is all part of the process to excelling on the waves. These spots serve up nutrient-dense meals to keep you energized and surf-ready.
Organic Market

body.alt: An image of coconuts

children.text: Every Saturday morning, local farmers and artisans gather to sell their produce and goods. It’s a great place to stock up on fresh fruits and vegetables for your stay. You’ll also find food stalls selling healthy treats like fresh coconut water (nature’s own sports drink) and homemade kombucha.
Don’t miss: Look for stalls selling local superfoods like chia seeds and raw cacao. They make great additions to your morning smoothie.
Cala Luna Restaurant
Nestled in the
Cala Luna Boutique Hotel
, this restaurant is a hidden gem for health-conscious surfers. With a focus on organic, locally-sourced ingredients, Cala Luna offers a menu that’s both nutritious and delicious.

Their farm-to-table concept shines in dishes like the quinoa and roasted vegetable bowl, packed with protein and complex carbs to fuel your surf sessions. For a refreshing post-surf meal, try their tuna poke bowl, featuring fresh, locally-caught fish.

Cala Luna also caters to various dietary needs with plenty of vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free options. Don’t miss their fresh smoothies and cold-pressed juices – perfect for rehydrating after a day in the waves.
Check out: the restaurant’s tranquil garden setting offers a peaceful retreat from the beach bustle.
Special Occasion Spots
Had an exceptional day on the waves? Celebrate with some of Tamarindo’s finer dining options.
Pangas Beach Club
With its beachfront location and stunning sunset views,
Pangas

is perfect for a special night out. The seafood here is excellent – try the seafood risotto, packed with locally caught shrimp, fish, and calamari. They also have a great selection of cocktails that perfectly complement the beach setting.
Surfer’s splurge: If it’s available, try the grilled lobster. It’s a treat that’s worth the splurge after a week of casual dining.
Seasons by Shlomy
For a true gastronomic adventure, head to
Seasons

. This restaurant takes Costa Rican dishes and elevates them to fine dining status. The menu changes regularly, but you can expect innovative dishes that showcase local ingredients in new and exciting ways.
Chef’s choice: Consider trying the tasting menu for a full tour of the chef’s culinary skills.
Late Night Options
After a day of surfing and maybe a bit of nightlife, you might find yourself craving a late-night snack. Here are some options that stay open late.
La Pachanga
This casual spot is open late and serves up satisfying comfort food.
La Pachanga
are known for their pizzas and calzones, which hit the spot after a night out. The portions are generous, perfect for satisfying those post-surfing, post-partying hunger pangs.
Hangover helper: Their breakfast menu, served all day, is perfect for those mornings when you might have overdone it the night before.
Tacos Tacos

When nothing but tacos will do, Tacos Tacos is there for you. Open late, especially on weekends, this spot serves up simple but delicious tacos. The al pastor (marinated pork) is a favorite, but their fish tacos are also excellent – a nod to Tamarindo’s coastal location.
Pro tip: Don’t forget to try their selection of salsas. From mild to fiery, there’s something for every palate.
Surf Camp Cuisine
If you’re staying at a surf camp, such as
Pura Vida
, you’ll be pleasantly surprised by the quality of the food. Many of Tamarindo’s camps are stepping up their culinary game.
Pura Vida Surf Camp from Rapture Surfcamps

At Rapture Surfcamps in Playa Avellanas, the culinary experience is as enticing as the waves. The camp’s chefs craft delicious meals using fresh, locally sourced ingredients, offering a delightful mix of cuisines.
Guests can enjoy a daily breakfast buffet and mouth watering dinners from Monday to Friday, all prepared in the camp’s kitchen. The full bar also serves refreshing drinks throughout the day.
Local Specialties to Try
While in Tamarindo, don’t miss the chance to try some
Costa Rican
specialties before or after your surf. Here are a few dishes to look out for:
Gallo Pinto

This mixture of rice and beans is a Costa Rican meal that’s a staple of the country, often served at breakfast. It’s usually accompanied by eggs, plantains, and sometimes meat or cheese. It’s a hearty, filling dish that’s perfect for fueling up before a morning surf session.
Casado
A typical lunch dish, casado consists of rice, beans, plantains, salad, and a choice of meat or fish. It’s a balanced meal that provides all the nutrients you need to get back on your board.
Patacones
These twice-fried plantain slices are a popular side dish or snack. Often served with black bean dip or guacamole, they’re crispy, salty, and utterly addictive.
Ceviche

While not unique to Costa Rica, the local version of this raw fish dish is not to be missed. Made with fresh, locally caught fish, lime juice, and vegetables, it’s the perfect light meal for a hot day.
Agua Dulce
This traditional drink is made from sugar cane juice and water. It’s sweet and refreshing, perfect for rehydrating after a long surf session.
Dining Etiquette and Tips
Here are a few things to keep in mind when dining out in Tamarindo:
Tipping
: A 10% service charge is usually included in restaurant bills. If it’s not, or if the service was exceptional, an additional tip is appreciated.
Reservations
: For higher-end restaurants, especially during peak tourist season, it’s a good idea to make reservations.
Dress code
: Most places in Tamarindo are pretty casual, but for finer dining establishments, you might want to change out of your board shorts and flip-flops.
Water
: Tap water is generally safe to drink in Tamarindo, but if you’re unsure, stick to bottled water.
Local specialties
: Don’t be afraid to try local dishes and ingredients. Costa Rican cuisine is diverse and delicious!
Timing
: Many restaurants in Tamarindo cater to the surf crowd, opening early for pre-surf breakfasts and staying open late for post-surf dinners.
Summing up: Surf and Turf

From pre-dawn breakfasts to late-night snacks, this surf town has a wealth of options to keep you well-fed and energized for those waves. Remember, good food is as much a part of the surfing lifestyle as the perfect wave. So don’t be afraid to explore and indulge in the local cuisine. After all, you’ll burn it off in the water tomorrow, right? Now get out there and enjoy both the surf and the local flavors.

From a physical health perspective, surfing is a fantastic form of exercise. It engages several muscle groups, particularly the core, legs, and shoulders, helping to improve strength and overall fitness. Additionally, the constant paddling required while surfing provides a great cardiovascular workout, enhancing endurance and promoting weight loss.

Why is surfing good for you? We know that physical activity, including sports, can have physical as well as psychological health benefits. But what are those particular to surfing? Is surfing better for you than, for example, cycling, karate or softball?
Let’s go over a few of the most beneficial aspects of surfing whether they be physical, mental or more spiritual in nature. Here are 10 reasons why surfing is good for you:
1. Surfing engages all your major muscle groups

Surfing can be considered a “complete sport” because it requires the use of all of the body’s major muscle groups. Simply paddling out to catch a wave engages your triceps, delts and biceps, traps, lats, obliques and stomach muscles — and that’s before you’ve even gotten to your feet. Getting to a standing position or “popping up” requires the use of your pecs, delts, triceps and biceps; followed by the muscles of your legs (mainly quads and gluts) and lower back once you’re standing. Staying upright and riding waves also brings your core muscles — stomach and mid and lower back — even more into play.
By engaging several muscle groups together surfing offers a full-body workout that enhances strength and muscle tone. So, grab your board and hit the waves for a fun and effective workout!
2. It’s a great cardio workout
In order to maintain a healthy heart as well as overall fitness, at least 2.5 hours of aerobic activity are
recommended per week

. As an energetic and intensive sport, surfing can contribute to maintaining good cardiovascular health. Since surfing is so much fun, for most surfers, 2.5 hours is a piece of cake. If you live near any good surf breaks, you may find yourself spending a lot more of your free time surfing than 2.5 hours a week!
3. Surfing might boost your immune system

Sustained or chronic stress is associated with many negative health issues, including physical ailments such as heart problems and brain tumors as well as depression and other mental illnesses. Short bursts of stress, on the other hand,
can bring health benefits

. Research from the University of Stanford suggests that acute stress, such as what you experience during thrilling adventure sports like surfing, may actually strengthen your immune system, improving recovery time and even helping your body protect itself from ailments including skin cancer.
4. Surfing makes you smarter??

Further research from Stanford shows that short bursts of stress can enhance memory and learning. Think of how being more alert can heighten cognitive ability. This is at least in part because cortisol, a stress hormone, can sometimes increase brain plasticity, a process in which the brain’s neurons form new synapses and prune old ones. So the thrills you experience catching a wave can help create more neural pathways in your brain, effectively making you smarter.
5. It improves balance and flexibility

The practice of surfing not only strengthens your muscles, but also makes you more dexterous and flexible. Many surfers practice yoga or Pilates to further improve both balance and flexibility, thereby making them better surfers. Stretching is also an important post-warm-up and post-surfing activity to prevent injury and improve performance.
6. You can lose weight while you surf

Intense physical activity, strength training and cardio vascular exercise all help you keep fit and trim. While “losing weight” may be a misnomer since muscle is heavier than fat, surfing will definitely help you burn fat and excess calories. The average surfer burns 400 calories an hour while surfing. Since surfing is so fun, you’re likely to spend more time doing it opposed to other grueling or boring forms of exercise.
7. Experience better mental health
Exercise, spending time outside, having active interests, being social, relaxing… all these aspects of surfing help improve one’s general mental health in a variety of ways:
Simply being outdoors and appreciating the beauty of the natural world can encourage a sense of wellbeing.
Being engaged in a hobby can improve your focus in life and provide a sense of purpose.
Surfing is also a social activity in which you can meet interesting new friends from many different walks of life.
Just being out in the ocean is both a relaxing and simultaneously invigorating experience.

Maintaining a positive state of mind and overall well-being is crucial for a fulfilling life. With the stresses and demands of daily life, it can often feel challenging to maintain a consistently positive mood. However, there are numerous ways to boost one’s mood and overall well-being. Engaging in regular physical activity, such as exercise or sports, has long been touted as a natural mood enhancer. Physical activity releases endorphins, also known as the “feel-good” hormones, which can significantly improve one’s mood and lead to a greater sense of happiness.
8. Surfing broadens your horizons
The search for newer, different and better breaks can lead you to all corners of the world. Surfing encourages you to experience new cultures and natural environments in places like
Bali
, California, South Africa,
Portugal
, Brazil, Australia, Hawaii, Morocco and
Costa Rica

. Meet fellow travellers and friendly locals the world over. Nothing broadens the mind like travel and getting to know new people. You may even meet a new work colleague or future spouse among your fellow members of the global surfing community!
9. Need perspective? Hit the waves!

Nothing grounds you like being knocked over and pushed underwater by a big wave. There’s no point in being angry, you saw how big it was and decided to surf it anyway. When surfing you can feel powerful in that you can ride something so awesome and vast, or you can realize how small you are and how incredible the Earth is. The surf isn’t something you fight — it’s something you try to harmonize with. Shouldn’t we be like that with other aspects of the world?
10. Make more friends

One of the most effective ways to meet like-minded individuals is by joining communities or groups centered around your interests.The sense of community within the surfing culture is a unique and powerful aspect that draws travelers in. Surfing is not just a sport or a hobby; it is a way of life, a culture that transcends boundaries. Traveling to different destinations to experience new surf spots allows surfers to immerse themselves in diverse cultures and connect with each other.

FAQs
What equipment do I need to start surfing?

To begin surfing, you’ll need some essential equipment. This includes a surfboard, a leash to keep the board attached to you, a wetsuit if you’re in colder waters, wax for traction on the board, and sunscreen to protect your skin from the sun’s rays. Beginners often find it helpful to start with a longboard, typically around 8 to 10 feet in length, as it offers stability and ease of paddling.
How do I choose the right surfboard?

Selecting the right surfboard is crucial for beginners. It’s recommended to start with a foam board or soft-top longboard as they offer forgiveness and stability while you’re learning. Consider factors such as your weight, height, and the type of waves you’ll be surfing when choosing a board that suits you best.
How important is proper paddling technique?

Proper paddling technique is essential for catching waves and navigating through the water efficiently. Focus on maintaining proper arm positioning, utilizing your core muscles, and timing your strokes with the rhythm of the waves. Practicing good paddling technique will greatly improve your surfing experience.
What are some basic safety tips for beginner surfers?

Safety should always be a top priority when surfing, especially for beginners. Always surf in designated areas with lifeguards present, and familiarize yourself with basic safety protocols such as identifying rip currents and understanding surfing etiquette. Start in smaller waves, respect other surfers in the water, and never turn your back on the ocean.
How can I improve my balance and stability while surfing?

Improving your balance and stability is key to progressing as a surfer. Practice pop-ups on land to build muscle memory and focus on keeping your weight centered with slightly bent knees while riding waves. Engage your core muscles to maintain stability and a low center of gravity, which will help you stay upright on the board.
What should I do if I’m having trouble catching waves?

If you’re struggling to catch waves, focus on positioning yourself correctly in the lineup where the waves are breaking. Paddle with enough speed to match the wave’s momentum, and time your paddling efforts to coincide with the wave’s peak. It may take some practice, but with patience and persistence, you’ll improve your wave-catching skills.
How do I progress from white-water waves to unbroken waves?

Transitioning from white-water waves to unbroken waves is a natural progression in surfing. Start by riding white-water waves to gain confidence and practice your balance. As you become more comfortable, move to the outside lineup and practice catching unbroken waves. Focus on efficient paddling and timing your take-off to match the wave’s speed.
How can I stay safe in the lineup among more experienced surfers?

When surfing among more experienced surfers, it’s important to be mindful and respectful. Always yield to those riding the wave and communicate with other surfers to avoid collisions. Start in less crowded areas to build confidence, and gradually move to busier lineups as you gain experience and become more comfortable.
What should I do if I get caught in a wave or wipeout?

Getting caught in a wave or experiencing a wipeout is a common part of surfing. Stay calm and protect your head by covering it with your arms. Hold onto your board to prevent it from hitting you or others, and wait for the wave to pass before resurfacing. With practice, you’ll become more adept at handling wipeouts safely.
How can I continue improving as a beginner surfer?

Improvement in surfing comes with regular practice and dedication. Take lessons from certified instructors to learn proper techniques and safety measures. Watch experienced surfers to observe their movements and strategies, and most importantly, enjoy the process of learning and mastering this exhilarating sport!

Nestled in the heart of Indonesia, Bali beckons travellers with its lush landscapes, vibrant culture, and enchanting traditions. As you embark on a journey to the “Island of the Gods,” understanding the local currency becomes an essential aspect of ensuring a seamless and enjoyable experience. In this guide, we delve into the intricacies of currency in Bali, shedding light on the official tender, practical tips for managing your finances, and the cultural nuances that make the Balinese rupiah more than just a means of transaction.

Join us as we navigate the diverse facets of currency in Bali, providing you with insights to make the most of your financial endeavours while immersing yourself in the unique charm that defines this captivating destination. Welcome to the world of Balinese currency – where every note carries a story and every transaction is a gateway to the island’s rich tapes.
What is the Currency Used in Bali?
The official currency used in Bali is the Indonesian Rupiah, abbreviated as
IDR
and symbolized as Rp. When quoting prices in
Indonesian Rupiah

, it is common to see the thousands implied, meaning that for example, when a price is written as “10.000” it actually represents 10,000 Indonesian Rupiah. Prices in Bali are typically represented with a comma separator for thousands and a period for decimals, so for instance, 100,000 Indonesian Rupiah would be written as “100.000”. When dealing with the currency, it is important to be aware of this convention to avoid confusion. Overall, the Indonesian Rupiah is the main currency used in Bali and is denoted by the abbreviation IDR and the symbol Rp.
Currency Converter
Currency Converter
by OANDA
Denominations of Rupiah

The Indonesian rupiah is the official currency of Indonesia and is known for its denominations ranging from small coins to larger banknotes. These denominations of rupiah are in use throughout the country and play a crucial role in the everyday transactions of the Indonesian people.

Understanding the denominations of rupiah is essential for anyone visiting or doing business in Indonesia, as it allows for a better grasp of the local currency and its usage in various contexts. Whether you’re a traveller, a businessperson, or simply curious about Indonesian currency, this article will provide valuable insight into the denominations of rupiah and their significance in daily life.
Rupiah Notes
The seven major banknote denominations in Indonesia are 1000, 2000, 5000, 10,000, 20,000, 50,000, and 100,000 Rupiah.

The latest series of banknotes was released in 2016, and notes come in denominations of 1000. It is advisable to be cautious when handling amounts of 10,000 Rupiah as counterfeit notes are common. Always ensure the authenticity of the notes when conducting transactions.
Rupiah Coins

The Rupiah coins in Indonesia come in denominations of 50, 100, 200, 500, and 1000. The Bali currency coins are made of either bronze, nickel or aluminium. These coins are not commonly used in everyday transactions but are still in circulation. The 1000 coin is the most commonly used and is worth approximately 7 US cents.

However, caution should be taken when using coins in busy markets as they are often overlooked and mistaken for smaller denominations. It is advisable to count your change carefully to ensure that you are receiving the correct amount. Despite their limited usage, Rupiah coins are still accepted as legal tender and can be used in smaller transactions.
What Currency Should You Carry to Bali?

When traveling to Bali, it’s essential to consider the best currency to take with you to ensure smooth transactions and favorable exchange rates. While the Indonesian Rupiah is the official currency in Bali, it’s also important to consider the convenience and accessibility of other currencies, as well as the potential for better exchange rates.

In this guide, we’ll explore the best currency options for travellers heading to Bali, taking into account factors such as exchange rates, fees, and accessibility. Whether you’re planning on using cash or cards, understanding the best currency to use in Bali will help make your trip more efficient and cost-effective.
How Much Do Things Cost in Bali?

In Bali, the average cost of food can range from $1.50 to $10 for a meal, depending on whether it is street food or at a mid-range restaurant. Transportation costs vary, but a one-way ticket on local transportation can be around $0.30, while renting a scooter can cost between $5 to $10 per day. Entertainment activities, such as visiting a temple or taking a surf lesson, can range from $5 to $50. It’s important to note that taxes and service charges are often added to restaurant bills, typically around 10% and 5% respectively. Overall, the cost of living and traveling in Bali is relatively affordable, with plenty of budget-friendly options available for food, transport, and entertainment.
What is the Best Way to Take Money to Bali?

When traveling to Bali, there are several options for taking money to ensure a convenient and cost-effective experience. First, it’s advisable to exchange some cash for Indonesian Rupiah upon arrival at a reputable exchange service. Another option is to use credit or debit cards, but it’s essential to be aware of potential charges and fees associated with international transactions. Opening a free account with fee-free banks like Revolut and Monzo or considering specific travel cards can be a good alternative to traditional banking and may offer better exchange rates.

For debit/credit cards, it’s crucial to inquire about foreign transaction fees and currency conversion charges. When it comes to the best currencies to bring, US dollars and euros are widely accepted and can be easily exchanged in Bali.

To make the most of your money in Bali, it’s advisable to practice smart financial behaviours such as bartering for goods and services and using two stashes for small and large purchases to avoid flashing large amounts of cash. By taking these measures, you can make your money go further and have a hassle-free experience when managing your finances in Bali.
Money-Changers in Bali

In Bali, authorised money changers with proper signage, security cameras, and money counting machines can be found in various popular areas. In Kuta, visitors can find reputable money changers along Jalan Legian and Poppies Lane. In Seminyak, authorised money changers can be found in the main shopping areas and along Jalan Raya Seminyak. In the vibrant area of Legian, money changers with proper facilities are located along the main streets and near popular tourist spots.

In Canggu, visitors can find authorised money changers with proper facilities in the main commercial areas and near popular restaurants and cafes. In Sanur, reputable money changers can be found in the main shopping and dining areas, offering a convenient option for visitors staying in this coastal town. In Ubud, visitors can find authorised money changers with proper facilities in the main tourist areas and near the popular Ubud market.

It’s important to look for authorised money changers that display proper signage, have security cameras in place, and use money counting machines to ensure a secure and reliable exchange process. Visitors should always be cautious and exchange money at reputable locations to avoid any potential scams.
Using you Bank Credit or Debit Card in Bali

When traveling to Bali, it’s important to understand the best ways to use your bank or credit card to access money and make purchases. With a variety of ATMs available throughout the island, as well as opportunities to use credit cards in many establishments, it’s crucial to be aware of any potential fees or exchange rates that may apply. By understanding the options for using your bank or credit card in Bali, you can make informed decisions and ensure a smooth and convenient financial experience during your travels.

If you find yourself in the unfortunate situation of losing your bank card in Bali, it’s important to take a few immediate steps to protect your finances and prevent any fraudulent activity. The first thing you should do is contact your bank as soon as possible to report the loss of your card, cancel it, and request a replacement. This will prevent anyone from using your card to make unauthorized transactions.

In addition to this, it’s essential to keep a close eye on your bank statements for any suspicious activity. If you notice any unauthorized transactions, report them to your bank immediately. To avoid any inconvenience while waiting for a new card, consider using alternative payment methods such as cash or mobile payment options.

By following these steps – reporting the loss, canceling the card, requesting a replacement, monitoring your statements, and using alternative payment methods – you can minimize the risk of financial loss and protect your account from unauthorized use.

FAQs
What is the official currency in Bali?
The official currency in Bali, as in the rest of Indonesia, is the Indonesian Rupiah (IDR).
Should I exchange currency before arriving in Bali, or is it easy to do so locally?
While it’s possible to exchange currency before arrival, it’s generally convenient to do so locally in Bali. Money changers and banks are prevalent, especially in tourist areas.
Are credit cards widely accepted in Bali?
Major credit cards are accepted in many establishments, especially in popular tourist areas. However, it’s advisable to carry some cash for places that may not accept cards.
Are there any currency exchange tips to keep in mind in Bali?
Use reputable money changers or banks to exchange currency. Be aware of the current exchange rates and check for any fees or commissions applied during the exchange.
Are there currency restrictions when entering or leaving Bali?
There are no strict currency restrictions for tourists entering or leaving Bali. However, there are limits on the amount of cash you can carry without declaring it at customs.
Surfing

is bigger than ever. Though an ancient Polynesian pastime imbibed with spirituality and cultural significance, it only began to emerge in global popularity during the late 1950s and early 60s. At this time, the modern surfing era began in its ancestral home of Hawaii, California, and Australia. Since then, surfing has gone through a few booms and even some lulls, but in terms of mainstream appeal and global spread, it is now experiencing unprecedented growth.

The concept of an ‘endless summer’ symbolizes surfers’ travels and quests for perfect waves, suggesting that the pursuit of surfing can lead to rich experiences and lasting memories that transcend time and place.
From Hawaii to
Bali
Portugal
to Russia, South Africa, Brazil, California, and Morocco, there are dedicated surfers, hobbyists, professionals, and casual admirers of
surf
culture.
Today, we ask the burning question:
Why is surfing popular?

body.alt: why is surfing popular
body.caption: Surfing in the 1950s, Corona del Mar, California. Pic: Orange County Archives

children.text: After much thought and a fair bit of research, we’ve come up with 25 reasons as to why surfing is so popular. Here they are in no particular order:
1. It feels like walking on water

What other sport lets you glide across the water without any additional tools except the board under your feet? Surfers can feel like they are flying, skating, swimming, and walking on water all in one good session.24.
2. Surfing is addicting

Call it an obsession, a compulsion, or just a healthy quest. The search for the perfect wave may always be just out of reach, but only a surfer knows the feeling of the unique joy and connection that comes from riding waves—the closer you get, the more fun you have!
3. It looks cool

This one may be a bit narcissistic, but if you also love watching other people surf, count yourself as a great appreciator of the art. Whoever you are — surfers and non-surfers alike — you have to admit that
surfing looks seriously cool

body.alt: why is surfing popular
body.caption: A cool Indonesian grom. Pic: Jared Hersch (Flickr CC)

children.text: 4. Surfing gives you a great reason to travel the world

It’s no longer just Hawaii, California, and Australia with active surf cultures. Anywhere there are decent waves, there’s bound to be some surf scene, which is becoming more so every day. Pick a coast, grab your board, and follow your dreams!
5. It provides an adrenaline kick
Like all active sports and nature-based activities, surfing gets those endorphins going. Still, few pursuits can match the adrenaline kick of catching a great wave.
6. It feels incredible to be out in nature, especially in the ocean
Being out in nature can be a healing experience, and this is never truer than when you’re out on the ocean. Feeling depressed? Hit the waves!

body.alt: why is surfing popular
body.caption: Pic: Stephane/Surf Girl (Flickr CC)

children.text: 7. Surfing is challenging
Nobody said surfing was easy. Virtually anyone can start surfing mild
beach breaks
with a bit of instruction. After your first few
surfing lessons
, you’ll be ready for your next challenge — and they keep coming!
8. It makes you feel alive
Getting out on the waves is as close as becoming one with Mother Nature. Surfing is exhilarating and healthy and helps to instill an appreciation for life and the natural world.
9. Surfing is excellent exercise
Looking for a fun way to get fit and enjoy numerous benefits? Look no further than surfing! It works for all the
major muscle groups
and gets you active like no other hobby.
How many calories does surfing burn?
10. Surfing takes you to some of the most beautiful places in the world

Find skiing too cold? Think hiking is too isolating? You can’t get much better scenery as nature sports go than a beautiful tropical beach or ocean vista. Surfing in a scenic holiday spot delivers excellent weather, a fun and social experience, plus incomparable views.

body.alt: why is surfing popular
body.caption: Scenic Bali surf. Pic: Egor Pasko (Flickr CC)

children.text: 11. You can surf in the best weather

Similarly, many of the world’s top surf spots are located where the best weather is. Though surfing is possible year-round and in many climates, it started in Hawaii and other Polynesian islands, so it’s ideally suited for warm, comfortable weather.
12. Surfing provides adventure
From the thrill of catching a wave to traveling the world and the challenge of finding “secret” surf spots, many aspects of the surfing lifestyle involve adventure.
13. People think
surfers are attractive

This may be a particularly shallow reason, but that doesn’t mean it isn’t true. Surfing is a healthy activity that helps get you fit, shows skill, and looks cool. Strangely enough, many people find these things attractive.
14. It’s an environmentally friendly sport

Surfers tend to care about the state of the oceans because they enjoy being in them and would rather not swim and surf in polluted waters. Surfing also gives you an awareness and appreciation of the natural world. All you need is a surfboard — and if you opt for one made from
sustainable materials
, you’re surfing ultra-green.
Big wave surfing
While it’s certainly not the kind of surfing that can be practiced by the masses, big-wave surfing has made a serious impact. The sheer spectacle daredevil big wave surfers like
Garrett McNamara
create regularly puts surfing on the front pages of many mainstream news sources.

body.alt: why is surfing popular
body.caption: Big wave surfer Jeff Rowley. Pic: Minnie Vuong/ Xvolution Media

children.text: 16. The surfer community

Surfing is more than just a sport; it’s a community sport. Surfers share a common love and lifestyle, bringing people together from different cultures, classes, nationalities, and walks of life. This sense of belonging is a powerful feeling that enriches the surfing experience.
17. The surfing lifestyle is cool
It’s not just the activity of surfing that is cool. The clothes, the music, the slang, and the surfing culture have spread worldwide and are popular with surfers and non-surfers alike.
18. Famous people who surf
From Agatha Christie to Anthony Kiedis of the Red Hot Chilli Peppers,
celebrities
have helped bring surf culture to the masses. Even President Barack Obama and Prince William knew their way around a surfboard.
19. Surfing is daring

Depending on your skill level and what kind of waves you like to ride, surfing can provide some serious thrills — if you dare. There aren’t many sports that can provide the type of excitement you experience rocketing through a perfect tube.
20. You get to experience nature — firsthand!
Besides the beach, the sea, the sun, and the sand, surfers sometimes interact with amazing animals, including dolphins, sea lions, and even whales!
21. Kelly Slater
Probably the
best surfer ever

, this 11-time ASP World Champion still rips at 43. Both the youngest and oldest person to ever become champ, Slater’s blend of skill, athletic prowess, charm, and good looks makes him the perfect surfing personality to help the sport grow. He’s also acted and appeared in several films and television shows (including a recurring role on Baywatch) and produced surf documentaries.

body.alt: Why is surfing popular
body.caption: Kelly Slater. Pic: Pedro Gómez (Flickr CC)

children.text: 22. Surfing has ancient connections.
The
origins of surfing

reach back to Polynesia several hundred years ago, before these cultures’ contact with Europeans. For Ancient Hawaiians, it was a spiritual art form. Whether they know it or not, each surfer is connected to this hallowed tradition.
23. Surfing has become a serious sport.
Since the 1960s, surfing has continued to grow as a sport, both professionally and in terms of amateur popularity. Pro surfers are now regarded as elite athletes with serious skills.

body.alt: why is surfing popular
body.caption: Ancient Polynesian surfers. Pic: mpora.com

children.text: 24. Technology
Advances in surfing tech have improved things like surfing equipment and
swell
monitoring, allowing surfers to reach and ride waves like never before.
25. Surfing is big business

Once a lot of money can be made from something, corporate interests will exploit it. Besides the many independent companies, surf camps, and schools, large corporations sell equipment, promote competitions, and endorse professional surfers.

Dive into the ultimate surf experience with Rapture Surfcamps, where every wave tells a story. Our surf haven awaits, offering a blend of excitement, skill-building, and relaxation. Choose Rapture Surfcamps for an unforgettable adventure.
Book your surf getaway now
and discover the perfect blend of adrenaline, skill development, and beachside bliss.
26. Overcoming fears and building resilience

Surfing is not just a sport; it’s a journey of self-discovery and growth. You’ll encounter challenges that test your courage and resilience as you ride waves. Overcoming fears and building resilience is an essential part of the surfing lifestyle. By facing your fears and pushing through them, you’ll develop a stronger sense of self-confidence and self-awareness.

Surfing teaches you to be present in the moment, to focus on the wave, and to let go of distractions. This mindset can be applied to everyday life, helping you to overcome obstacles and achieve your goals.
27. Discovering new breaks and experiencing different environments

One of the most exciting aspects of surfing is discovering new breaks and experiencing different environments. From the warm waters of Bali to the rugged coastlines of California, each surf spot offers a unique experience. Surfing takes you to some of the most beautiful places in the world, where you can immerse yourself in nature and connect with the local culture. Whether you’re a seasoned surfer or just starting out, exploring new surf spots is a great way to challenge yourself and expand your horizons.
28. A sport for everyone

Surfing is often perceived as a sport for the young and adventurous, but it’s for everyone. Regardless of age, fitness level, or background, surfing is accessible to anyone willing to try it. Surf camps and schools offer lessons and guidance for beginners, while experienced surfers can always find new challenges and opportunities to improve. Surfing is a sport that promotes inclusivity and diversity, welcoming people from all walks of life to join the surf culture.
29. Why surfing is a way of life

Surfing is more than just a sport or a hobby – it’s a way of life. The surfing lifestyle is built around a deep connection with nature, a passion for adventure, and a commitment to personal growth. Surfing culture is a vibrant and diverse community that values creativity, sustainability, and mutual respect.

When you love surfing, you’re not just riding waves – you’re embracing a way of life centered around freedom, joy, and a sense of belonging. Whether you’re a seasoned surfer or just starting out, surfing can transform your life and inspire you to live in harmony with the natural environment.

WHAT OUR GUESTS HAD TO SAY ABOUT THEIR STAY AT RAPTURE SURFCAMPS
The largest country in Central America,
Nicaragua
, is home to a diverse range of attractions. From colonial cities like Granada and León to spell bounding natural wonders that includes volcanoes,
beaches

and more, Nicaragua tourism has something to offer everyone. Travelers should be aware of the limitations of healthcare in certain areas and that ambulance services provide transportation and basic first aid only.

How to Get Around in Nicaragua

Once you are in the country, you can get around Nicaragua by road(buses/care rentals), boat or by air. The Managua airport serves the majority of domestic flights that help you reach tourist spots all the way across, to the other end of the country. It’s recommended to pre-arrange transportation or use authorized services to ensure safety and convenience.
If you plan to stay in Nicaragua beyond 30 days, it is important to obtain an international driving permit, as a Canadian driver’s license is only acceptable for a limited duration.
Getting around Nicaragua using shuttles

Shuttle travel in Nicaragua is a popular and convenient way to explore the country’s various tourist destinations. With its many advantages, taking a shuttle can offer a hassle-free and seamless journey for travelers.

One of the main benefits of opting for a shuttle is the convenience it offers. The shuttle driver will pick you up directly from your hotel, saving you the trouble of navigating unfamiliar public transportation or hailing a taxi. Similarly, upon reaching your destination, the shuttle will drop you off at your desired location, eliminating the need for additional transportation arrangements.

Moreover, shuttle travel is straightforward and efficient. Unlike public buses, shuttles operate on fixed schedules, ensuring timely departures and arrivals. This means that you won’t have to worry about unexpected stops or delays along the way. Instead, you can sit back, relax, and enjoy the journey without any interruptions.

Whether you are looking to explore the colonial city of Granada, relax on the Pacific coast in San Juan del Sur, or venture to the natural wonders of Ometepe Island, taking a shuttle can be a convenient and stress-free option. So, sit back, relax, and let the shuttle transport you seamlessly between Nicaragua’s beautiful tourist destinations.
Getting around Nicaragua on public transport

Public transport in Nicaragua offers several options for travellers to explore the country affordably. One of the most popular modes is the iconic chicken buses, which are former American school buses. These brightly coloured vehicles are often crowded but provide an authentic and lively experience. Chicken buses stop frequently along the routes, allowing passengers to easily hop on and off at various destinations. While they may lack comfort levels, they are extremely affordable, making them a favourite among budget travellers.

Another mode of public transport in Nicaragua is shuttles. These are shared vans or minibuses that provide a more comfortable and convenient option compared to chicken buses. Shuttles have fixed schedules and fewer stops, allowing for a smoother and quicker journey. Although slightly more expensive than chicken buses, shuttles offer a more relaxed and comfortable travel experience.

The average cost for using public transport in Nicaragua varies depending on the distance and mode of transportation. Chicken bus fares are generally very affordable, ranging from $0.50 to $2 per trip. Shuttles, on the other hand, can cost between $5 and $20 per journey, depending on the distance and route.

When traveling in Nicaragua, public transport options like chicken buses and shuttles provide affordable and convenient ways to explore the country. Despite the trade-offs in comfort levels, these modes of transport offer unique experiences and opportunities to interact with locals.
Getting around Nicaragua by plane

If you’re planning to travel by plane in Nicaragua, there are a few important considerations and tips to keep in mind. While it may not always be necessary to travel by plane within the country, there are certain situations where it can be the most convenient option.

When it comes to safety precautions, it’s important to choose reputable airlines and ensure that they adhere to international safety standards. Check the reviews and ratings of the airlines before making a booking. Additionally, follow all instructions and guidelines provided by the airline staff, especially during take-off and landing.

The best time to schedule flights in Nicaragua is typically during the dry season, which runs from November to April. This is when you can expect clear skies and minimal chances of weather-related delays or cancellations. Avoid scheduling flights during the rainy season, as heavy rainfall and storms can disrupt flight schedules.

It’s also worth noting that Nicaragua has several domestic airports, including in major cities like Managua, Granada, and Leon. If you’re planning to visit different regions of the country, flying can save you a significant amount of time compared to traveling by land. However, keep in mind that smaller planes may have weight and size restrictions for luggage.

Before traveling, make sure to check flight availability and book your tickets in advance, especially during peak travel seasons. This will help ensure that you secure your preferred dates and avoid any last-minute issues. Overall, by considering these factors and following necessary safety precautions, traveling by plane in Nicaragua can be a convenient and efficient option.
Getting around Nicaragua using car rentals

When it comes to exploring Nicaragua, rental cars may not be as popular as in neighbouring countries like Costa Rica. However, for those seeking a comfortable and well-planned option for getting around, renting a car can be a viable choice.

Renting a car allows you the freedom to explore Nicaragua at your own pace and venture off the beaten path. Whether you want to visit the colonial city of Granada, hike up volcanoes like Cerro Negro, or relax on the stunning beaches of San Juan del Sur, having a rental car gives you the flexibility to create your travel itinerary.

It’s important to note that driving conditions in Nicaragua can sometimes be challenging, especially in rural areas where roads may be unpaved or poorly maintained. Additionally, it is advisable to have a thorough understanding of local traffic laws and regulations before hitting the road.

Overall, while rental cars may not be as prevalent in Nicaragua as in other countries, they can still be a comfortable and well-planned option for travellers looking to explore this beautiful Central American nation. If you are planning to drive, make sure you have your International Driver’s Permit(IDP) and carry it along with your original license.
What is the best time to visit Nicaragua?
The
best time to visit Nicaragua

would depend on your preferences. The peak tourist crowd visits Nicaragua between the months of November and May. This is the dry season in the country and is flocked by visitors from all over. It is also when the rates for accommodation and other activities are pricier.

If you are looking to save on this trip and want to avoid the crowds, the ideal time to visit Nicaragua would be from May to October. However, we must warn you that this is also the wet season in the country. This would mean encountering rain and thunderstorms. The weather would also limit your trekking opportunities in some of the areas. But there is a lot you can still do if you decide to visit Nicaragua during the wet season. The accommodation is the cheapest during this period. The rainy season can impede overland travel and disrupt essential services such as roads and bridges.
Food in Nicaragua
Nicaraguan cuisine
is known for its vibrant flavours and unique combinations. One of the most traditional dishes in
Nicaragua is Gallo Pinto
, a hearty and flavourful mix of beans and rice that is served at almost every meal. This dish is usually accompanied by plantains, eggs, and sometimes cheese.

Another popular snack in Nicaragua is the quesillo, which consists of a homemade tortilla filled with melted cheese, pickled onions, and sauce. It’s a delicious and quick bite that can be found in street stalls all over the country.

For a more substantial meal, don’t miss out on trying the nacatamales, a traditional dish made with masa (corn dough) filled with pork, rice, vegetables, and spices. These large parcels are wrapped in banana leaves and steamed until tender.

To complement these delicious dishes, make sure to sample the local beers and rum. Tona beer and Victoria beer are popular choices amongst locals and visitors alike. For a stronger drink, try Flor de Cana rum, a Nicaraguan classic known for its smooth flavours. With its diverse flavours and unique combinations, Nicaraguan cuisine is a must-try for any food lover. From the staple beans and rice to the mouth-watering Gallo Pinto, quesillos, and nacatamales, there’s something to satisfy every palate in this vibrant country. Don’t forget to wash it all down with a refreshing Tona or Victoria beer, or indulge in the rich flavours of Flor de Cana rum.
Accommodation in Nicaragua

When it comes to accommodation options in Nicaragua, there is something to suit every traveller’s preferences and budget. From vibrant hostels to luxurious hotels and unique local rentals, the country offers a wide range of choices.

For budget-conscious travellers looking to meet fellow explorers, hostels are a popular choice. Hola Ola Hostel in San Juan del Sur is known for its friendly atmosphere and beachside location, while Viavia Leon in León is a great option for socializing and discovering the city’s vibrant culture. In Granada, De Boca en Boca offers a charming and authentic Nicaraguan experience.

For those seeking a bit more comfort and privacy, hotels are readily available throughout the country. Private rooms with breakfast can start at around $20 per night, but prices may vary depending on the location and included amenities. Many hotels also offer additional amenities such as swimming pools and restaurants.

Alternatively, for a more unique and immersive experience, consider renting a local property. Nicaragua has a range of rentals available, from cosy guesthouses to spacious villas. This option allows travellers to stay in local neighbourhoods and enjoy a more authentic atmosphere. Regardless of your accommodation choice, Nicaragua’s diverse options cater to different preferences and budgets, ensuring an enjoyable stay in this beautiful country.
Nicaragua’s Currency

When traveling to Nicaragua, it’s essential to familiarize yourself with the local currency, the Nicaraguan Cordoba (NIO). The exchange rate is approximately 1 USD to 35 NIO(this rate may vary). While US dollars are widely accepted in popular tourist areas, it’s advisable to carry some Cordobas for small, off-the-beaten-path establishments.

Exchanging money is relatively easy in Nicaragua. Banks and official exchange offices can be found in larger cities like Managua, Granada, and Leon. These establishments offer competitive exchange rates and are considered the safest options. Airports also have money exchange facilities. However, the rates may be competitive and so we advise exchanging only a small amount. Additionally, Nicaragua’s Sovereign Security Law and its Terrorism and Money Laundering law allow for broad interpretations, which can lead to arbitrary convictions.

ATM machines are prevalent and allow you to withdraw Cordobas directly from your bank account. However, it’s important to inform your bank of your travel plans beforehand to avoid any issues with card transactions. At the same time, be advised that your bank may incur additional charges and fees on withdrawals.

Carrying small denomination US dollar bills is also advisable, as they will be accepted in most places, especially tourist areas. However, it’s always a good idea to have Cordobas on hand for smaller purchases or when visiting more rural areas, where acceptance of US dollars may be less common.
By being prepared and aware of the currency exchange options and practices in Nicaragua, you can ensure smooth and hassle-free transactions during your travels.
Top 5 Cities to Visit Nicaragua
Nicaragua will spoil you places to visit while you are in the country. If you are in Nicaragua on a short trip, we recommend visiting these top 5 cities in Nicaragua:
1. Granada

Granada is a vibrant and colourful city located by the Lake Nicaragua. It houses some of astound Spanish colonial architecture that has been well-preserved. Laden with rich history and vibrant markets, Granada is a culturally rich city you must visit when you are in Nicaragua.

Explore the iconic yellow Cathedral of Granada, go down the picturesque cobblestone streets or just spend a few relaxing moments by Lake Nicaragua. A very popular tourist attraction in Granada is the boat tour to the Islets of Granada. These are small islands that were formed as a result of volcanic activity in the region. Granada offers a unique blend of history, culture, and natural beauty, making it a must-visit city in Nicaragua.
2. León

Another city in Nicaragua known for its colonial architecture, León houses one of Central America’s largest cathedrals – the UNESCO World Heritage León Cathedral. Explore the city’s rich history by visiting the Museo de la Revolucion, dedicated to the Sandinista Revolution, or take a tour of the Ruben Dario Museum, honouring Nicaragua’s national poet. For adventure enthusiasts, nearby volcanoes like Cerro Negro offer the opportunity to hike or even try volcano boarding, a thrilling activity unique to the region.
3. Ometepe Island

Ometepe Island is situated in Lake Nicaragua. A natural paradise, the island is known for the twin volcanic peaks  – Concepcio and Maderas. The island offers stunning landscapes, lush forests, and pristine beaches. Visitors can hike to the summit of either volcano for breath-taking views, explore archaeological sites, or simply relax on the black sand beaches. Ometepe Island is a haven for nature lovers and a perfect destination for those seeking tranquillity and outdoor adventure.
4. San Juan del Sur

This is one of the popular beach towns in Nicaragua. Its golden sandy beaches attracts surfers, sun-seekers, and party enthusiasts. There is a myriad of choices for water activities you can enjoy here. For a panoramic view of the town and the coastline, hike up to the Cristo de la Misericordia statue. San Juan del Sur offers a perfect blend of relaxation, adventure, and vibrant nightlife.
5. Matagalpa

Nestled in the northern highlands of Nicaragua, Matagalpa is a charming city known for its coffee plantations, cloud forests, and cooler climate. It is an ideal destination for nature lovers and eco-tourism enthusiasts. Explore the lush Selva Negra Cloud Forest Reserve, home to a diverse range of flora and fauna, or visit coffee farms to learn about the process of coffee production. Matagalpa offers a tranquil escape from the hustle and bustle of the city, allowing visitors to immerse themselves in nature’s beauty while savouring some of the country’s finest coffee.
Nature and Wildlife in Nicaragua

Nicaragua, a country blessed with stunning landscapes, is a haven for nature and wildlife enthusiasts. From volcano boarding in Cerro Negro to exploring the beauty of Laguna de Apoyo, there are endless opportunities for adventure and encounters with unique species.
One must-try activity is volcano boarding, where thrill-seekers slide down the active Cerro Negro volcano on a board. It’s an adrenaline-pumping experience like no other.
Laguna de Apoyo, a peaceful crater lake, offers a serene escape with its crystal-clear waters and lush surroundings. Take a dip, kayak, or just unwind by the shore.

For a glimpse into Nicaragua’s volcanic wonders, Masaya Volcano National Park is a must-visit. Witness the spectacular lava flow and explore the otherworldly landscapes created by the active volcanoes.

Ometepe Island is a natural haven boasting vibrant biodiversity. Birdwatchers will be thrilled by the abundance of colourful birds, including parrots and toucans. The island is also home to fascinating creatures like howler monkeys and capuchin monkeys.

Whether you’re seeking adventure or tranquility, Nicaragua’s nature and wildlife will leave you awe-inspired. Get ready to immerse yourself in unforgettable experiences and encounters with the diverse flora and fauna that make this country truly exceptional. Pay close attention to local media reports, especially during emergencies such as earthquakes or other natural disasters, to stay informed about local conditions and government alerts.

swells
of beginner-friendly beaches like San Juan del Sur to the powerful breaks of world-renowned spots like Popoyo and Maderas, there’s a wave for everyone. You will find a host of
